req.title: Interest accrual (idempotency variant)
req.statement: A savings account shall accrue interest daily using the tier rate and credit interest monthly on the last business day. The operation must be idempotent: duplicate submission with the same Idempotency-Key must return the original result.

doc.reasoning: R-FIN-091 has three testable dimensions: (1) correct interest accrual and monthly crediting on a valid savings account (positive / EP), (2) idempotency: a duplicate POST with the same Idempotency-Key must return the original response without double-crediting (positive / STC), (3) a zero-balance account sits on the lower boundary — it should receive £0 interest with a ledger entry of 0 (boundary / BVA), and (4) a missing or malformed Idempotency-Key header must be rejected with a 4xx error without writing any ledger entry (negative / NEG). A fifth case covers the decision table for tier-rate selection: accounts in different balance tiers must accrue at the correct tier rate (positive / DT). Together the five cases cover all major partitions, state transitions, boundary values, and error pathways described in the requirement.

tests.name: test_interest_credited_on_last_business_day
preconditions: A savings account (ACCT-001) exists with a positive balance in tier-1
The system date is the last business day of the current month
No interest has been credited to ACCT-001 this month
A valid unique Idempotency-Key is available
actions: POST /cron/interest with header Idempotency-Key: <unique_uuid> and body {"account_id": "ACCT-001"}
Record HTTP response status and body
Query ledger for ACCT-001 entries created in this month
expected: HTTP 200 returned
Response body contains interest_amount > 0
Exactly one ledger entry of type INTEREST_CREDIT exists for ACCT-001 this month
Ledger entry amount matches interest_amount in response
trace: R-FIN-091: interest credited monthly on last business day
tests.executable:
import pytest
import uuid
from <helper_fn> import api_client, get_ledger_entries, reset_account_interest

def test_interest_credited_on_last_business_day(<fixture_last_biz_day>, <fixture_tier1_account>):
    account_id = <fixture_tier1_account>["id"]  # e.g. "ACCT-001"
    reset_account_interest(account_id)
    idem_key = str(uuid.uuid4())

    resp = api_client.post(
        "/cron/interest",
        json={"account_id": account_id},
        headers={"Idempotency-Key": idem_key},
    )

    assert resp.status_code == 200
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["interest_amount"] > 0

    entries = get_ledger_entries(account_id, entry_type="INTEREST_CREDIT")
    assert len(entries) == 1
    assert entries[0]["amount"] == pytest.approx(body["interest_amount"], rel=1e-6)

tests.name: test_duplicate_idempotency_key_returns_original_result
preconditions: A savings account (ACCT-002) exists with a positive balance
A first POST to /cron/interest with Idempotency-Key IK-42 has already succeeded
The ledger contains exactly one INTEREST_CREDIT entry for ACCT-002
actions: POST /cron/interest again with the same Idempotency-Key: IK-42 and same body
Record second HTTP response status and body
Query ledger for ACCT-002 INTEREST_CREDIT entries
expected: Second request returns HTTP 200
Response body is identical to the first response body
Ledger still contains exactly one INTEREST_CREDIT entry (no double-credit)
Account balance has not increased a second time
trace: R-FIN-091: duplicate submission with same Idempotency-Key must return original result
tests.executable:
import pytest
from <helper_fn> import api_client, get_ledger_entries, reset_account_interest

IDEM_KEY = "IK-42-state-transition"
ACCOUNT_ID = "ACCT-002"

def test_duplicate_idempotency_key_returns_original_result(<fixture_positive_balance_account>):
    reset_account_interest(ACCOUNT_ID)

    payload = {"account_id": ACCOUNT_ID}
    headers = {"Idempotency-Key": IDEM_KEY}

    first = api_client.post("/cron/interest", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert first.status_code == 200
    first_body = first.json()

    second = api_client.post("/cron/interest", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert second.status_code == 200
    second_body = second.json()

    assert second_body == first_body

    entries = get_ledger_entries(ACCOUNT_ID, entry_type="INTEREST_CREDIT")
    assert len(entries) == 1, "Idempotent call must not create a duplicate ledger entry"

tests.name: test_zero_balance_account_accrues_zero_interest
preconditions: A savings account (ACCT-003) exists with a balance of exactly 0.00
The system date is the last business day of the current month
No interest has been credited to ACCT-003 this month
A valid unique Idempotency-Key is available
actions: POST /cron/interest with header Idempotency-Key: <unique_uuid> and body {"account_id": "ACCT-003"}
Record HTTP response and ledger state
expected: HTTP 200 returned
Response body contains interest_amount == 0.00
Exactly one ledger entry of type INTEREST_CREDIT exists with amount 0.00
Account balance remains 0.00
trace: R-FIN-091: daily accrual using tier rate (lower boundary at zero balance)
R-FIN-091: ledger entry per account even at zero interest
tests.executable:
import uuid
from <helper_fn> import api_client, get_ledger_entries, get_account_balance, reset_account_interest

def test_zero_balance_account_accrues_zero_interest(<fixture_zero_balance_account>):
    account_id = <fixture_zero_balance_account>["id"]  # balance == 0.00
    reset_account_interest(account_id)
    idem_key = str(uuid.uuid4())

    resp = api_client.post(
        "/cron/interest",
        json={"account_id": account_id},
        headers={"Idempotency-Key": idem_key},
    )

    assert resp.status_code == 200
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["interest_amount"] == 0.00

    entries = get_ledger_entries(account_id, entry_type="INTEREST_CREDIT")
    assert len(entries) == 1
    assert entries[0]["amount"] == 0.00

    balance = get_account_balance(account_id)
    assert balance == 0.00

tests.name: test_missing_idempotency_key_returns_4xx
preconditions: A savings account (ACCT-004) exists with a positive balance
The Idempotency-Key header is intentionally omitted from the request
actions: POST /cron/interest WITHOUT the Idempotency-Key header and body {"account_id": "ACCT-004"}
Record HTTP response status and body
Query ledger for ACCT-004 INTEREST_CREDIT entries
expected: HTTP 4xx (400 or 422) returned
Response body contains an error message referencing Idempotency-Key
No INTEREST_CREDIT ledger entry is created for ACCT-004
Account balance is unchanged
trace: R-FIN-091: idempotency — missing key must not process or partially credit
R-FIN-091 error pathway: reconciliation variance prevented by rejecting bad requests
tests.executable:
from <helper_fn> import api_client, get_ledger_entries, get_account_balance, reset_account_interest

def test_missing_idempotency_key_returns_4xx(<fixture_positive_balance_account>):
    account_id = <fixture_positive_balance_account>["id"]
    reset_account_interest(account_id)
    balance_before = get_account_balance(account_id)

    resp = api_client.post(
        "/cron/interest",
        json={"account_id": account_id},
        # No Idempotency-Key header
    )

    assert resp.status_code in (400, 422)
    body = resp.json()
    assert "idempotency" in body.get("error", "").lower() or \
           "idempotency" in body.get("message", "").lower()

    entries = get_ledger_entries(account_id, entry_type="INTEREST_CREDIT")
    assert len(entries) == 0, "No ledger entry should be written on bad request"

    balance_after = get_account_balance(account_id)
    assert balance_after == balance_before

tests.name: test_tier_rate_applied_correctly_across_balance_tiers
preconditions: Three savings accounts exist: ACCT-T1 (balance in tier-1), ACCT-T2 (balance in tier-2), ACCT-T3 (balance in tier-3)
System date is last business day of the month
No interest credited this month for any of these accounts
actions: POST /cron/interest for ACCT-T1 with unique Idempotency-Key
POST /cron/interest for ACCT-T2 with unique Idempotency-Key
POST /cron/interest for ACCT-T3 with unique Idempotency-Key
Compare each returned interest_amount against expected monthly accrual
expected: ACCT-T1 interest_amount matches balance * (1.0/100) / 12 within tolerance
ACCT-T2 interest_amount matches balance * (1.5/100) / 12 within tolerance
ACCT-T3 interest_amount matches balance * (2.0/100) / 12 within tolerance
Each account has exactly one INTEREST_CREDIT ledger entry
trace: R-FIN-091: tier rate used for daily accrual
tests.executable:
import uuid
import pytest
from <helper_fn> import api_client, get_ledger_entries, reset_account_interest

TIER_CASES = [
    ("ACCT-T1", 5_000.00,  1.0 / 100 / 12),
    ("ACCT-T2", 25_000.00, 1.5 / 100 / 12),
    ("ACCT-T3", 75_000.00, 2.0 / 100 / 12),
]

@pytest.mark.parametrize("account_id,balance,monthly_rate", TIER_CASES)
def test_tier_rate_applied_correctly_across_balance_tiers(account_id, balance, monthly_rate, <fixture_last_biz_day>):
    reset_account_interest(account_id)
    idem_key = str(uuid.uuid4())
    expected = pytest.approx(balance * monthly_rate, rel=1e-4)

    resp = api_client.post(
        "/cron/interest",
        json={"account_id": account_id},
        headers={"Idempotency-Key": idem_key},
    )

    assert resp.status_code == 200
    assert resp.json()["interest_amount"] == expected

    entries = get_ledger_entries(account_id, entry_type="INTEREST_CREDIT")
    assert len(entries) == 1
